title: The Sea is Red but the Earth is Still Blue
display_title: The Sea is Red but the Earth is Still Blue
wikitext:
[]: The Sea is Red but the Earth is Still Blue....?             Quote Post by jojojoker » Mon Aug 02, 2010 6:55 am It's an obvious sight that in NME the ocean has become a red, lifeless pool. That being said, how is it that the Earth is still blue? The colour of the earth kinda fluctuates. It goes from blue-ish with a red tinge purpleish to down right what a normal earth would look like; blue water and all. Does that mean that the red ocean is only partial to one section of the world?

[]:             Quote Post by Marduk » Mon Aug 02, 2010 7:36 am This belongs here , me thinks.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 02, 2010 8:06 am In the context of the first two images, one should be lead to believe that everything about the air on planet Earth is the same. Originally it's the sky the provides the ocean water with it's familiar blue hue, however in NME we see that the skies are still a perfect blue, meaning only the ocean has changed. The blueish hue we see at the edge of the Earth is the atmosphere being looked at a different angle. As for the third image, I can't quite pick out the context. Is that when Nerv-02 explodes with Unit-04?

[]:             Quote Post by jojojoker » Mon Aug 02, 2010 8:18 am yeah, moments before you see the explosion spreading. Now, I just find it strange that compared to the image of earth at the end of EoE Although both earths have red oceans, NME's earth looks much more normal from afar. Just something i noticed.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Mon Aug 02, 2010 11:03 am Because some of Rebuild is retouched paintings from NGE and some is original content.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Mon Aug 02, 2010 11:29 am On the third picture the ocean looks black, but it's more like very dark purple. There's no blue.

[]:             Quote Post by jojojoker » Mon Aug 02, 2010 11:50 am Jinroh wrote: On the third picture the ocean looks black, but it's more like very dark purple. There's no blue.

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Mon Aug 02, 2010 1:46 pm In addition as it seems the red ocean surrounds Japan, shouldn't the sky be red and not blue? Haha just kidding.......

[]:             Quote Post by Natsuka_Chie » Mon Aug 02, 2010 2:17 pm Why would the sky be red? Water reflects the color of the sky, not the other way around. As for the third picture, for me it seems the green/bluish parts are land, and the dark one near the right edge is ocean.

[]:             Quote Post by konfunoodle » Mon Aug 02, 2010 3:10 pm Wow I must seem retarted. Just ignore that haha... In 1.0 the scene where it's an over view of Japan(when we see all tge lights go out) tge ocean didn't seem red from what I remember. Though, it was night time in that scene.

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Mon Aug 02, 2010 4:35 pm jojojoker wrote: I'm pretty sure the blue sections, i.e. the majority of the picture, is ocean...The darker spot is a mystery though

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Aug 02, 2010 6:26 pm Jinroh wrote: Isn't it continental north america?

[]:             Quote Post by facespace » Mon Aug 02, 2010 10:27 pm Have they explained why the waters are still red?

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Tue Aug 03, 2010 10:08 am facespace wrote: Have they explained why the waters are still red?

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Aug 03, 2010 6:25 pm Water is red. Earth is blue. I hate Rebuild. And so do you.

[]:             Quote Post by Sun Stealer » Tue Aug 03, 2010 11:21 pm I can't quite make it out, but it looks like Nevada gains some waterfront real estate in the rebuild timeline.

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Wed Aug 04, 2010 2:06 am facespace wrote: Have they explained why the waters are still red?

[]:             Quote Post by jojojoker » Wed Aug 04, 2010 3:16 am Nice observations people. Yeah I wasn't sure why the land mass would be so blue. I guess if it's lake probably the lake water isn't red like oceans...haha confusing

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Aug 04, 2010 2:32 pm The red stuff is the Angel-equivalent of LCL from Adam's explosion being in the oceans (Angels explode into blood in Rebuild, as you know) so the water in the atmosphere which is distilated water should be all right -> the sky is still blue, and blue + red = purple. That explains the first two pictures. Lakes and subteranean water should also be fine as Adam's remains spilled into the oceans only and they're mostly filled by rain. There must've been some normal water left somewhere, I mean, Shinji & co must drink something. Plus if the reain was infested, the preseration center would have a big fat roof. You can't really see much on the last one, I mean, there's blue where NORTH AMERICA should be. It's probably some cloud stuff

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Thu Aug 19, 2010 6:13 pm Ok. We still don't know what is making the water red, it could be blood or some other mystery substance. But in the case of the color differences in the shots, it seem that they are either day shots or night shots. The light from the sun would reveal the red in the water of the oceans, but at night, the oceans would appear black from space due to lack of light. The purple would come from the sun angle during dawn and dusk allowing a certain amount of light to reflect off the water, making it seem purple. The sky has nothing to do with the color of water. It is what makes up water that determines its color. The Hydrogen and Oxygen atoms absorb a lot of red light in the visible spectrum, as well as a little bit of green light, thus leaving blue to reflect back, creating the blue color. The sky being blue has to do with the atoms in the atmosphere absorbing and reflecting certain light within the visible spectrum. So, at night, there is not reflection of the red light off of the tainted water, leaving it to look black (or normal) from space.
